Other people left without anything. So that part's up to kind of you guys. I did a podcast about this other day when I was wrestling. My dad would come to wrestling practice and there was a coach. We had a really, really good coach, right? And coach would be in there and be training all of us. And what's interesting is that the coach taught everybody the same things, right? He taught me and everyone in the room learned the same moves. We learned the same moves, the same things, the same practice, the same amount of hours. Everything was the same. And then after practice was over, my dad would come. He'd get off work, he'd come over and then he worked with me afterwards on different techniques and movements. And then when I got home, I'd go home and I'd climb. We had a rope climbing my back yard, I'd climb the rope, and then we had wrestling mats on my deck and I'd bundle up my snow clothes. We'd go outside on the deck and my buddies would come over and wrestle again. And I look at everyone in our team. Our team did really, really good. But there were five or six people that ended up becoming state champions and national placers and beyond. And I remember my junior year after winning the state title, we had a bankway. And my dad came to the bankway and he said something that had such a profound impact on me. He said that a coach can get anybody up to this level. That's what good coaches do. They can get anybody from wherever they're at and get them up to this level. So the difference between someone who's good and someone who's a champion is actually like 15% that they're doing it on their own. Okay, so us collectively, we know what we're doing. We've done this over and over and over again for a decade now. And I can get you guys all to this level. But if you guys to go from being like, you're doing well to having the impact you want on the world, the extra 10% to 15% is on you guys. I have a friend and the other day I was listening to his podcast and he defined an entrepreneur in a way that I'd never heard before. But ever since he said it, it started just, it got me so excited. So this was his definition. It's actually Ryan Moran. I know, yeah, Ryan said it's an event and it had such a profound impact on me. I listened to like 10 times in a row and I was like, oh my gosh, like that is so huge. She said an entrepreneur is someone who took personal responsibility for a problem that was not their own. Okay. So what does that mean? Okay. Most people when they're going through life, the majority of the world, right, they see a problem that comes to them. They're like, ah, and they blame who they blame the government and they blame the school system. They blame this. They blame people. That's what 99.9% of the entire population does. If you're on Facebook at all, you see that all the time, right? Everyone has these problems. They complain and they whine and they want to blame somebody else. Us weird entrepreneurs are here today. Okay. And you've got to be weird because it means you're passionate about whatever it is you're doing that you signed up for some goofy software. You probably read one or two of my books. You're showing up here in Boise, Idaho in the middle of almost winter. It's cold today, huh? It gets, you guys are a little bit weird, okay? Which is good. But entrepreneurs, they see these things and they see these problems instead of being like, oh man, like, God, like this government is messing us up or whatever that thing might be. They look at that thing, that problem, and they're like, I'm going to take personal responsibility for that thing and I'm going to fix it. Okay. That's not normal. Just so you guys know. That's something weird amongst all of you guys, amongst all of us. Okay. A couple examples from my inner circle that I think is a really good story. So this is Pamela Weibull. How many of you here know who Pamela Weibull is? Couple of you guys. Cool. So Pamela's main. So Pamela was a normal human being. She woke up. She was a kid. Then she went to school. Then she went to become a doctor. She was going to medical school. She had all sorts of fun time learning how to fix people and cut them open and everything. Right. They do medical school. I assume that's what they do. I have no idea. Then when she's in medical school, one of her close friends who's in medical school with her, committed suicide. She was like, oh my gosh. Then she had another friend commit suicide. Then she became a doctor and she had people around her committing suicide. Eventually, what she realized, I didn't know this, but doctors have the highest suicide rate of any profession. Almost double the number two thing. It's an epidemic. People that are, and it's not only bad because the doctors commit suicide. The doctors have these amazing patients that they're working with. And also, those patients lose a doctor. When she was here in Boise, a month ago, we had her last meeting when she was here. She gave a little presentation. She dedicated to a doctor here in Boise who worked with special needs kids who took his life two weeks prior. She's not only the doctor who loses his life, not only did their family get destroyed. There's also all these special needs kids who lost a doctor. It's like this huge epidemic. Pamela is seeing this happening over and over and over again in her profession. People she loves. What does she do? The normal person say, man, it's the medical industry. They're screwing people up. The schooling system's wrong. All these kind of things and they would blame other people. But Pamela looked at that and said, you know what? This problem right here, this is my problem. I'm going to figure a way to solve it. I don't care how much money it costs. I'm going to solve this problem. She started trying. She didn't know how she was going to do it. She started on this mission. I got to fix it. This is now my problem. I'm going to take personal responsibility. I'm going to fix it. She started thinking after thinking after thinking. She's written books. She's got all sorts of stuff. Just on and on and on. Now she is literally saving thousands and thousands of doctors' lives around the world. She has just retreats. She has all stuff. It's all because she took that personal responsibility, that thing. That thing wasn't her responsibility and made it her own. That was the day she became an entrepreneur. That was the day she changed the world. Another person here. This is Kailin. Let all of you guys know Kailin. She's probably about 5'1". She's like this tall. She's like 185 pounds. So 5'1 girl, 180. She was overweight for her whole life. She struggled. They've helped last check over 100,000 women who have paid them to be inside their programs. So yeah, give them a round of applause. So same kind of thing. There's a response to this. She could have been blaming a thousand different people for this problem. Instead she's like, no, I'm going to take personal responsibility. I'm going to fix this thing. Next one here, this is Annie Grace. And Annie, her story is so cool too. And that's what makes you different than the rest of the world. That's why I love waking up and helping and serve entrepreneurs, is because you guys are all different. You're looking at those things and saying, like, what is the problem in the world? Like, what is the thing that I need to fix for myself and for other people? And then you risk your time, your energy, your money, your talents, with no guarantee of success. There's not a doctor where you get a degree and you're guaranteed to get a job making whatever. There's no safety net. We're just like, OK, let's go do this thing. And you're jumping off the cliff. So these are a note sheet for you. It's interesting. So first off, I want to stress everything you're learning during this process, you guys. It's not something that worked for me like 10 years ago. This is all super timely. Like in the last 30 days, I've written two new webinar pitches. First webinar pitch I did about three weeks ago. It took me 48 hours from the idea of the webinar until the actual webinar started and had all sorts of issues and errors and everything. I spent a lot of time initially mapping things out in this before I ever opened up any slides. It was fascinating as I was doing it. And Steve kind of noticed and we were like, we've been telling everyone basically what we do a lot in the fat events is going slide by slide by slide. It's not the best way to do it because that's not how we do it. The way we do it is we map out where we're going all initially through here and then we have it all sketched out. Then I go from the slides and I'm looking, okay, what is the slide here? What's the image here? And then that's how we build the slides out. And then tomorrow when we start diving the slides, you'll have the entire framework and we can go way faster and you'll have a lot more stuff done faster. So that's kind of this. So as we're going through everything I'm talking about today is going to be parts of this and we're going to go deeper and deeper and deeper. So right now I'm going to be starting kind of up here at the initial framework of where we are going. So how many of you guys have ever read the book Ready, Fire, AIM? By Michael Masterson? Yeah, that's cool. So there's that book. There's a couple other... There's a lot of good business books out there that I always talk about, like how to go from zero to a million dollars, million to ten, ten to fifty, fifty to a hundred. And I've read most of them and they're all like really good books. But they all focus on like the business structure. Like first you need to hire a manager and then they need to have seven people and then those seven managers hire seven men and like it's all like hiring and structuring. I don't know about you guys, that part bores me to death, right? Like I'm a marketing guy, I just want to sell a bunch of crap. So I'm like, hey, in my world how do I go... Like how do we go from a million to ten, ten to a hundred? Like what was our process from a marketing standpoint? All those guys teach all the business structuring stuff. I don't really care about that. But from like actually like you as the entrepreneur, like what is it that you guys need to do? And what is it that you should be focusing on? Okay, so most entrepreneurs should not be focusing on management and hiring team, all that kind of stuff, right? Because most of us, I'm guessing, don't love that thing, right? I mean, it's just like to sell stuff and create stuff all day. Yeah, so I'm going to focus on the fun part. You can hire people to build the other infrastructure. Does that sound good to you guys? Yeah. Okay, all right, so we're going to go through this. So the first goal is how do you go from zero to seven figures? Then we got seven to eight and then eight to nine. Okay, I've got three circles here that we're going to be going through. Okay. This is the shift to go through these phases. The thing that typically keeps businesses from going from here to here to here to here is not like lack of ideas or products. It's usually you as the entrepreneur getting bored. Okay, that's what normally happens. So what normally happens, and I'm guilty of this, it was a decade of my life, is I would go and I'd have an idea for the most amazing thing in the world, right? And I'd go and I'd create it, I'd build that, build the cell system, I'd get everything done and we'd launch it, we'd make a bunch of money, I was like, sweet, and I was like, hey, what's the next thing? And I'm like, okay, what's the next thing? And we'd build the next thing, right? Then we launched that and we'd build the next thing, we'd keep doing thing after thing after thing after thing. How many of you guys in this room have more than one company? How many of you guys have more than two? One, three, four, five. So five is the worst? That's actually not too bad. We had some with like 14 last time. Okay, so let me walk you through my process. So I was the same thing. So I built my first company up and it was doing really well. It timed out a million dollars a year in sales. And I was like, okay, this is working, this is kind of cool. And then I was like, but I want to make more money. So my brain is entrepreneurs, like, okay, I got a million dollar business. If I just create a second one, guess what's going to happen? I'll have two million dollars. I'm like, that's going to be the great thing, right? So I did that. So I did the first business, created the second business, and also put an end tension. And this one starts going up, I'm like, oh my gosh, and we hit a million dollars in sales in this one. And look back over here, what happened in this one? It's like, crap, okay, that didn't work. Okay, well, let's see. Maybe it's just I need more business. So then I went from one to two. And so I was like, okay, with this one, this one, and then a third one. So we built the third one, we go over here, start focusing, focusing, focusing. And this one starts building, this is awesome, awesome. And then these two dropped down and it was the same thing. Okay, for like six years of my life, just so you guys know, I was stuck at $3 million a year for six years of my life, six years, okay? And I went from having one business to three, to the like genius Russell, who doesn't figure things out very fast. The next phase is we launched 12 new companies in one year. I was like, well, maybe one, I managed to do 12, and then I get like three of them to pop. I don't know who that, so we launched 12 companies one year. And what was amazing is that one of those companies exploded, and all the rest suffered, and we made $3 million a year. And I was like, this is horrible. And that's where most of the entrepreneurs get stuck at, is that we get, this part is fun for us, like the idea and the creation, the building, and they launch it, and then we're like, oh, what's the next thing? And we always want to move on, okay? So what I found is that those who move on next thing, next thing, next thing, never actually like incrementally grow from here to here to here. They always stay about in here. I did for almost a decade. It wasn't until I started shifting, and the fear again comes back to us as the entrepreneurs, right? Like we have so much fun in the creation part of it. And so I remember when we launched ClickFunnels as a company, at that time, we were getting, we were about $3 million a year or so, and it was like, we'd launched this thing, and we launched it, and it was doing really well, and then my entrepreneur ADD brain was like, okay, what's the next thing? What's the next thing? But I had all these partners, and customers and things, and they're like, that were in here, and I was like, okay, I gotta focus, I gotta like spend, I can't just walk away from this and launch the next business. But I had fear because like, me and entrepreneur, my creativity is in the first part, right? And so what's interesting that I kind of figured out, and you can see on the bottom here, is as you shift from here to here to here, like really the big secret is that your focus is the entrepreneur, it's called the creativity switch. It's me shifting my focus on what I'm focusing on that grows each level, but keeps me engaged, okay? So this makes more sense as I kind of go through it. So the first phase here, if you're going from zero to a million bucks, is all about figuring out two things. We call this the what and how, okay? What is it that you are actually selling that the market actually wants? And the second thing is like, how are you selling that thing? What's the sales system that's in place to sell that thing? Okay? And so this is where a lot of people get stuck and they try to figure out the what and how, the what and how. So how do you know when you figured out the right what and the right how? You know you figured out the right what and how, because as soon as you figure that out, you go from zero to a million dollars almost overnight. Like, all the barriers just disappear and it's really, really fast to get there. Okay? That's how you know. Dan Henry, good example. He struggled for like seven or eight years, read the Doc Oumseekers book, figured out how to figure a hook, figured out how to do a thing. Launched this thing in five months from zero to a million dollars. Like just, as soon as you figured out, it just goes really, really fast. Okay? The problem with most of us entrepreneurs is we come in and we have this idea of what we want to sell. And then we go and put it out there to the market. We try to sell it and then like, the market doesn't really want it. But it's like, our baby, we love it. We created this thing. It's so cute. And we go out and try to sell it, right? But the market doesn't want it. I did a podcast last week actually about this a little bit. And I was liking it to God and I'm going to share that, but it ties both ways, right? In the podcast I talked about how most humans, especially here in America, right? And like we, for whatever reason, we think that like our job is to like convince God that he needs to change what he does, right? It's really funny. So like, no, like you should believe this and we should do, and like it's really weird. It's like, look, the reality is if you believe there's a God, like my goal is not to convince him of what is right. My goal is to feel like, what does he believe? And then conform my will to his, right? I'm not going to do a church lesson right now because that's not the point of this thing. But it's important to understand like how that works because the exact same thing is true in the marketplace, right? Someone's coming out to the marketplace and we're like, you need to buy this thing. The market's like, no, I don't want it. Like, no, you have to buy it. Like this is awesome. Like we keep trying to shove down the throne. Like we don't want it. Okay. And that happens to so many entrepreneurs. They keep trying to keep trying. They're like, I created this thing. I spent so much time and energy building and developing it. We keep trying, trying, trying, and then the market doesn't want it. Our job as entrepreneurs is not to go and try and shove something in our market's throat. Our job is to figure out what does the market actually want and then conform to that. Okay. Rule number one of direct response marketing is sell what people are buying. Like find out what they actually want and then we create that. Like that's what we're going to be giving for them. Okay. So if you're struggling, if you're not hitting, if you're not at a million bucks right now, it's because you are selling, like you're selling what people don't want. Okay. So you have to figure out like what does the market actually want? If I can figure out what they want, then I can create that and then oh my gosh, it blows up really fast. Okay. So that's number one is figuring out what they want. And number two is like how to sell it. And so this event is going to be going through both of those. Okay. Stephen is going to be working a little later on today with you guys like how to structure the offer. Okay. Some of you guys like reason why people aren't giving you money is your offer sucks. That's it. You fix the offer. You fix the what, what they actually want. It gets sexy. You make it. We talk about like turning up the sexiness. Like you make it sexy. Like I need that thing. Then it's easy. They'll give you money. He's going to talk about offers and how to create offers and there's been offers and products. A lot of times as marketers and entrepreneurs, we think about products. It's like it's not a product. It's a sexy offer that's amazing. That's why I can take almost any business and I can make it sexy because I can turn it from a product to an offer and then all of a sudden it becomes sexy. He's going to walk to you. You've got to do that in a couple of hours. So that's the first thing to figure out what it is that people actually want. And second one is like how, how do we sell it? Okay. Now as we sell stuff, there's different ways to sell things, right? The traditional way that most people do is like one-on-one selling. This is what Stephen talked about earlier, where you're going and you're knocking doors, you're doing phone calls, whatever. And typically with one-on-one sales, you can sell like higher ticket things, but you're stuck with how much time you have. So then the next phase is like let's hire more people. So in an earlier business, eight years ago, I had 60 sales guys in a room calling people all day long and it was horrible. How many of you guys are sales people in this room? Okay. Sales people are like the most high maintenance human beings on earth. I love you all. I'm the same way. So I had 60 grown men kids that I was managing. And this is how sales person works. And the best ones that are like the amplification of this, they make a whole bunch of money like Monday through Friday. And then on Saturday, they spend all of it, not like some of it. Like it's gone. And then Monday they show back up again and you're like, hey, how's it going? And they're like, hey, I'm broke. And they start working it, which is really nice, right? But they're also like emotional like babies. Like if you like, like it's just really hard and I hated it. And so like, yeah, we skilled that way. We'd hope it would be a whole removal people calling. But man, like, like if the wind blew weird that day, like it was just, oh, so I'm not a big fan of like scaling like build like 800 people on a sales team. Like you can do it that way. The better way though is to figure out selling one to many. Okay. Which is like this whole process. Okay. Now I'm not the best one on one sales person. In fact, I'm probably the worst. If you come to me and like try to, I want me to sell you something face to face. I'll probably just give it to you. Cause I'm like awkward and okay, cause I'm not good at that. I'm super introverted like, like face to face. And so like, I don't, I'm not good at that. So like, I never figured out that way to sell and I don't like it. So I learned how to do is stand up on this thing like this in front of a bunch of people where they can't talk back to me or introverted Russell can feel like I'm a man and I can speak and I can create a craft of presentation in a perfect way that gets the masses to move and gets the masses to buy stuff. Right. And somebody has heard this story before like the first time I ever even understood how this world worked. I went to, to arm and more in one of his big seminars and amid this event, I thought it was an internet marketing. What was it? Intermarketing conference, but I'd never been to one before. I'm there with my laptop and my glasses on a shaved head. And I'm like, you know, trying to be like this internet dude, because I didn't want to talk to people. That's why I started internet business. Right. So I'm sitting there like trying to take notes and the first presenter gets on stage, he starts doing his thing, he's talking. And the end of it, he sells a product and I was like, what? And then like, also I look around, all these people start popping up, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, and they start running to the back of the room. And he was selling $2,000 course and I'm like doing the math. I'm counting two, four, six, eight, ten, twelve, fifteen. So I'm like, that's made $86,000 in an hour. I think, what in the world? How did he do that? And then he says, next guy gets up, this guy's even better, he's doing his whole thing, boom, boom, boom, and he sells them for $5,000 and I watch the room jump up and people are running, jumping over tables, finding the back, like five, ten, fifteen, twenty, twenty, twenty, like 250, a quarter million bucks in an hour. And so the next person, and like that's the back day when that's how events were pitched, like every single speaker sold something. And I'm watching this process in about, you know, two or three hours in this thing. I have to learn that skill. Because if someone can stand on stage and talk for 90 minutes and make a quarter million dollars or a million dollars or more, like that's something I've got to figure out. Like that's worth learning, right? Selling to the masses. Because that's better than having 60 sales guys all on the phone trying to close it. But if I can just close it all at once, like that's way better. And so I started this journey to figure out how, like how do you actually sell that way? Because if I'm going to scale it, I don't want to scale people, why is that? That's super scary and painful and like, ugh, I want to scale like smart through leverage, right? And so I started, I went on a journey to try to figure this thing out. So I went to my very first event. To me, I heard the story too. I got on stage and I remember because like, I'd seen all these other guys do their presentations and kind of like made fun of them. Like there's, like, I don't know, it's kind of bugged me that they did it and they were always like, it's not going to be this, it's not going to be this. And they do these weird sales tricks and I'm like, ah, it's so cheesy and weird. So I remember getting on stage and I did my presentation and I like, I taught so, like, I was like, I'm going to blow these guys' minds. I know more than any of these guys. I'm going to teach like the best stuff I got. So I taught everything I had. People are like taking notes like crazy. I remember at the end I got the point and said, I'm not going to insult your guys' intelligence like the other speakers. My product just cost $997. And I was like, come to the back of the room and sign up. And then it stopped and nobody budged. Nobody moved. And it was like the most awkward, I could hear that crickets like, errr. And the dude usually was supposed to crank up the music afterwards, but he was even like confused and so we just sat there awkwardly with like nothing and I was like, I kind of like, bye. Like, oh my gosh. And I walked off the room and then like some guy came like, that was really awesome. Like I learned so much good stuff. And I was like, why are you buying? He's like, well, you gave me everything I needed. Like I'm good, man. Thank you. And I was like, huh. And I remember feeling so awkward. I like I went and ran to the elevator and went to the room. I had three days to be at this event. I sat in the room and I was eating coconut shrimp and Hagen Dazs ice cream. I watched every single movie possible because I was too scared to go back down in the room because I didn't want to ever see like they knew that I like had just, yeah, blow my opportunity. And I was like, I will never speak from stage. Like I'm already awkward and nervous anyway. I don't want to do this. It's just a horrible thing. And I'm never going to do it again. And I didn't for a while. And then I went back and I started again, I started seeing more people doing it. And then about this time when people started doing it more online, but back then it was through tele seminars. Like we were all doing everything on phone. And so I was like, why can you tell seminar? Like no one sees me. I can just jump on the phone and just talk. So I started doing tele seminars and it started. And at first nothing really happened. I tried again and again, eventually I started making some sales. I started watching what other people were doing. And I started going to events and I started like trying to understand the art of this, of this game. Right. And then eventually I started getting better at it. And then I started going to events and speaking. And then I started actually making some sales. And then I decided for a little while I was going to be a public speaker, which was kind of a joke my wife and I had because she was just like, you're so awkward and nervous and shy. I was like, yeah, but these guys are making so much money. So I went on this on like the seminar circuit for about two, two to three years, getting on stage in front of people. And what's cool about it is I had a chance to watch some of the best people in the world do this over and over and over again. And I'm like in the back taking notes on like what they said and how they said and how they're anchoring the room and how they're like all these things. And I asked them afterwards like, where did you learn that? I learned from this guy. So I hired that guy and again, three years of this getting better and better and better at the point where I could stand on any stage. I know look at that room and exactly how many people in the room I tell you within like a thousand dollars, how much money I was going to make during that presentation. Because I knew exactly how my clothes were converging. I knew everything I was going to say. I knew what jokes I was going to say. What I was going to say and how I was going to say everything perfectly to a T and we started cranking out money. And it was awesome. Until one night, it was a Sunday night. It was like 10 30 or 11 o'clock and I was at the Boise Airport sitting there. I was the only person in the airport waiting to take a flight out to Atlanta or somewhere to go speak. And I was sitting there thinking about my wife and we said new twins at the time and I was like, this sucks. Like I don't want to be on the road traveling. Like this is the opposite of like this dream, right? And so I texted her that night. I was like, hey, I'm going to quit speaking. She's like, what do you mean? I'm like, I'm going to I'm done. She's like, you can't be done. That's how we make money. I'm like, oh yeah. Well, I'm going to try to figure out how to do this online. And so I did. So I quit speaking. That was the last time I was ever on the circuit. And then it came back like, how do we do this online? Again, it was tele seminars first and came webinars. And I've done this so many times over and over and over and over again. And we kind of perfected the system. And it's not something I invented. It's me learning from the best speakers in the world over a decade period of time and testing this on literally tens of millions of people have seen our webinars and testing over and over and over again. So everything you're learning is it's true. It works. It'll give you the ability to sell one to many and in a lot of platforms. What do you guys think of that one? You were there at it? Was it cool? It was pretty loud. So he had like, he had like 3,000 people in the room. And I told him, I was like, hey, I'm going to get probably 15% of the people to run over and buy something. He's like, that's not possible. He's like, usually we get a few sales. I'm like, I need like 40 sales guys, many tables. And they're like, we still have the resources. I'm like, are you kidding me? We did the presentation, closed over a million dollars in sales. Sure enough, everyone's sprinting through the room. Like, it was crazy. And Grant Cardone's in the front like, oh my gosh, like what's happening, right? So their next events in February, they got 8,000 people in a basketball stadium. And I'm speaking at it, and they're like, whatever you want, we will do. I was like, I need a table next to every single exit, every single door. And so anyway, it's going to be really fun. But it works, right? Like, this process works. And you guys get good at it. Like, it'll give you the ability to persuade people, influence people, give people to buy, give people to break their false belief patterns that keep them from actually having success. What happens is it's easy to sell something, but people's false belief patterns keep them from actually having success with it. If you do this process right, not only do you make a crap ton of money, it also will destroy their false beliefs and give them the ability to actually do what it is you're teaching them, which is more important, okay? Because no one wants to make a whole bunch of money and then leave at the end of the day where your customer doesn't actually have success, okay? So that's the how. I'm going to be learning a lot of that throughout this week. So that's the biggest key here is in the first, like, going from zero to millions, figuring out the what and how, what am I selling it, how am I selling it. When you figure that out, you'll know you figured out because you went from zero to million bucks, like, almost overnight. Like, it just, it scales really, really quickly. If you're struggling, it's because you're missing one of these. Either what you're selling the market does not want or how you're selling it is wrong. Okay, but you fix those two things, it just goes fast. Okay? Other things to be aware of in this. How many of you guys read the expert secrets book? Okay, I hope so. This is all the stuff you figure out in this phase. And as soon as you figure that out, that's going to explode. Boom. Okay? Now we shift over to the next phase here in the business. Now this is where us entrepreneurs screw everything up because we shift over here and also we go to this part of the business. We're like, this person is not as fun. Like, I want to create new stuff. And this is why most of us, we come back and like, this part is so exciting and romantic and fun. We shift back to this and we get stuck in this thing. I have friends. I've been with this now, man, 14, 15 years. So I've been in the 14, 15 years, like I've been around almost everybody in almost every industry doing this kind of thing. Most people never break past about here. And it's because of us as entrepreneurs. We get the addiction of the creation of the thing. And so we shift back. So when ClickFunnels came out and we launched it, I was like, this is so awesome. This is amazing. I was like, hey, what's the next thing? And the team was like, no, please focus. We can actually do something big here. But I'm like, what am I going to do? I'm going to go crazy. I need something for my creative outlet. And so we came back here and all of a sudden it was like, OK, if you instead shift your creativity from trying to figure out what the business is to shifting your creativity to like, what are the creative things I can do to actually market this business and sell more of it, then how does that change? And I was like, oh, cool. OK, so my goal is ClickFunnels. This thing, I put it all, this is the hardest thing, by the way, you guys. This is the thing that you entrepreneurs can do that nobody else can do. You can't outsource this piece. There's no one on earth that's going to be excited and passionate about your thing to get it from where it's at into orbit. After your idea is into orbit, you can plug in any more on MBA and they can run the thing and it'll just sit in an orbit, keep going around in circles. None of our ClickBank friends are in here, right? So ClickBank says Melon, he doesn't work there anymore. So we can make fun of him. So ClickBank is the company. When I got started, ClickBank was like the 500 pound gorilla, right? And their entrepreneurs got this thing off the ground, put it into orbit, it started floating, and then they hired a whole bunch of business people. They plugged the whole thing in and these guys have been destroying that business for a decade. Like, it's insane, but it's in orbit, so it just keeps going around. OK, so you can hire people eventually, but you can't do this. Like, this is like love and passion and you going like, right? Because if you don't have that, getting a business off into orbit is hard. After it's in orbit, anybody can run it, but it takes an entrepreneur. It's passionate to figure this part out. Then it's in orbit and they come over here. So now I'm over here and I'm like, OK, business in orbit is doing good. Like, I can create more things, but only if the goal of it is to sell ClickFunnels. Like, that's got to be the goal, right? And so we started focusing on some other things. One of my mentors is a guy named Jay Abraham. Anybody here who studied Jay Abraham? All right, a couple of you guys. So Jay said there's only three ways to grow a company. How many of you guys know what those are? Everyone has like a million ways to grow a company, but you broke it down. It's like there's only three ways to grow a company. Number one is what? More customers. OK, number two is what? Get those customers to give you, let's see, mo money. Is that a thing? More money. And number three is given by more often. OK, more often. So I looked at that and I was like, OK, more customers. In my funnel world, what is that? Like, I need to get, we call them acquisition funnels. How do I create a funnel to acquire more customers? Acquisition, OK? Acquisition funnels. So I make cool. So if I've got this ClickFunnels thing, I want to grow it from a million to 10. I got to get more customers. So what are the cool acquisition funnels that I can create that gets more people into the front door? OK, so then I'm like, well, OK, well, a lot of people buy ClickFunnels. They don't understand what it is. What if I wrote a book that teaches people like the basics of like funnel psychology and stuff like that? And I can put my creative energy into like creating a book that's like the guide that teaches people funnels. And I can create a funnel to sell the thing. And also, like, my entrepreneur, like ADD, can be focused on this thing. But what's cool about it is that after someone buys it, the next thing they do is they buy ClickFunnels. And so now everything that, all the effort I put into this gets people into the next thing. OK, so I'm allowed to do that. There's a lot of crap that I've created I'm not allowed to sell because it doesn't bring people into ClickFunnels. OK, but there's a lot of stuff I can sell now here, right? And then like a year later, I'm bored again. I'm like, I need something else. I'm like, I'm going to write another book. So I'm going to write another book, right? Which now, and I created this acquisition funnel, I'll get more customers in. But the whole goal is just to get people to get into ClickFunnels, right? So I'm shifting my creative focus into what are the frontend funnels I can create that bring people into my business that I built? Not, I'm going to build a whole new business. It's very similar, but it's very different as well, OK? All right, acquisition number two then. So we got acquisition funnels. Then we try to get people to spend more money. So I call this an ascension funnel. And then to ascend. OK, ascension funnel. So inside of my main thing, ClickFunnels, it's like, OK, people pay $97 a month, or we have a $297 a month level. We're coming out at the next big event in Orlando. We're launching the next phase of it, which is like, I can't tell you, totally yell at me. It's awesome though. Oh, so cool. And so my next thing is, OK, well, how do I get people to ascend from $97 a month to $297 to the next phase? And I'm like, OK, well, what kind of funnel do I need to create? How many of you guys have done last year's Funnel Hacking Live? OK, who remembers when I was on stage and I did this whole thing where we made fun of the Fusion Soft? And we had the Confusion Soft t-shirts. And I made fun of them. It was really funny. And then I was like, hey, everyone, if you go right now and upgrade from $97 a month to $297 a month, you get a free t-shirt. It's going to be awesome. And let's all ascend together. Everyone went crazy. And the entire room jumped up, ran the back of the room, and everybody as a collective whole, almost like 100% of the room. It was like 85% of the room. And then I collectively said, OK, I'm going to go from paying $97 a month to $297 a month, and everybody upgraded for a free t-shirt. It was amazing. Right? It was good. So I got to focus my creativity on that, on getting people to ascend. And then we took that same presentation offline. 50% of the people who watched that presentation upgrade from here to here. So I got to focus my creativity on ascending people. Anyway, but now I got to focus my creativity on that, which all helps build the initial business. And the third one then is how do we get people to spend more money? So you've got the foundation for it. Okay. All right. So this is the foundation for this piece right here is about creating a mass movement of people. Right. This is the whole selling one to many and having a huge impact. Okay. So there are two types of businesses I think in this world. There's like transactional businesses and then there's like business that cause change. Okay. The transactional business, I'm less excited for. There's like listing stuff on Amazon or Craigslist or eBay and you're making transactions. That's one thing. The businesses I'm interested in are ones that are actually making big impact on the world. Like those are the ones I spend most of my time. That's where most of you guys are at. Where you can actually have an impact on someone's life beyond just like a transactional product they got. They buy soap off Amazon. You made some money and it's like it ends. Right. So that's why my focus is always on this. Like how do we how do we shift from just being a product to like a movement? Okay. When we first launched ClickFunnels it was interesting. It was actually Rachel. You were there at the time. So I went to a network marketing event and and they this company sold a software program. Not even a good software program with this software program or this event and they're probably like, I don't know, three or 4,000 people in this room. And I'm sitting in the room and I'm watching all these people get up on stage talking about the software thing and they're crying. I was like, why are they crying? It doesn't make any sense. Why is everyone crying? And like everyone's crying and all these things and then people are on stage and everyone's getting awards and all that stuff. And I'm like, why are they crying? This is software that melts things and the mail to people. It doesn't make any sense to me. And like for three years I'm watching this whole process. I'm so confused. And people that were in this room they spent more money like the software and the company is supposed to make you money. People that spent more money on their flights to get to the event to stand in line and cry about this thing than they'd ever made in the company the entire time they've ever been. Some of them for four or five years, right? And I was like, why are they all crying? I'm so confused. And David Fry, who's one of my buddies, was sitting next to me and he leaned over and he said, you know what they've done here? I'm like, I have no idea. But they're all crying and I'm so confused. Please tell me. And he was like, they're not selling software. He's like, they've created a movement. I was like, oh my gosh, it's true. We have the movement. The product doesn't matter as much. Hopefully you've got an amazing product. But that's what they've created. And this is like a year or two before we launched ClickFunnels. But that kept ringing through my head. I was like, OK, how do you create a movement? And that time I started geeking out and started studying a lot of fun things, mostly like how people build cults because it's a fascinating thing. How do people build cults and all this sort of stuff? And I started studying from a whole bunch of different levels. I'm like, how were cults built? How are religious movements built too? How are political campaigns built and all sorts of stuff in between, right? And as I was writing this book, The Expert Secrets book, I was actually in Kenya on a little car that was bouncing around, reading The True Believer and a couple other things like in this car, I'm trying to take notes. My wife had passed out, she's sleeping next to me. And I'm looking at all these things. And I'm highlighting three or four books I'm reading through and going through stuff. I'm circling different things. And as I'm going through everything, all of a sudden I realized there is this really interesting unique pattern that's happening in religious movements, cults, political campaigns, successful businesses across the board that all of them had. And I'm sitting there in this little thing and I drew this little picture. And I drew the little thing. It didn't look as good. I have a guy who takes my chicken scratch in terms of it looks awesome. But I drew this thing and I was like, oh my gosh, they all have it. And I was like, is that true? And I started looking at thing after thing after thing, all the ones I was aware of. And as I was looking, all of them had this same pattern. Now I'm a big pattern guy. The cool thing about a pattern, as soon as you define a pattern and you realize what it is, then you can take each of those pieces of the pattern and you can amplify it. Until you know the pattern, if you know the pattern, it's just kind of wandering in the dark. But as soon as I realized the pattern, I'm like, OK, every mass movement from the beginning of time until now has these three things. They have a charismatic leader. They have a future-based cause. And they have a new opportunity. As soon as I was aware of that, I'm like, OK. Well, now that I know those things are there, how do I amplify the attractive character, the charismatic leader? How do I amplify this? How do I amplify this inside of my business? And then the next two or three years was all of us. First was me doing it in our company and seeing these huge things. And then my inner circle, we've got 100 entrepreneurs in the inner circle, some of you guys are in it. I started sharing these things with everybody there. And then all of them started going out and doing it in all their businesses. And we saw the same thing happening in weight loss and finance, the finance industry, and all these different markets. And it was like, oh my gosh, as soon as you know it, you can amplify it. So this is the pattern. It's very simple. It's very easy. And we kind of walk through all of them. And then it'll give you as ideas on how you can amplify this for yourself. If you say, I'm just going to sell a product, again, you're transactional. As soon as you ship from, like, I'm selling a product, so I'm creating offers that are trying to cause some kind of movement, some kind of change, that's when your business will explode. All right, so I'm going to start with the charismatic leader and attractive character. So this is where this phase of the business typically starts, right? What happens for most people is you start over here. And how many of you guys, like, when you were younger, like, you wanted to sell the thing you're selling right now? Yeah, like, I never sat there. I was like, I want to teach people how to launch funnels or make it. Like, that was never a thing, right? What happens is, like, you start learning something. And for all of you guys, it's different. Some of you guys, you learned finance, weight loss, you learned health, you learned, you know, biohacking. You know, like, everyone's got something different, right? There's something that, like, you came in as a student and you started, you gravitated towards something that got you excited. And you got, you started geeking out, like, oh, my gosh, like, this is the coolest thing in the world, right? You started studying and learning. And, like, you did everything you could, like, fill yourself up on that thing, right? How many of you guys have gone through that process before in your life? Right? Yeah. It's, I don't know about you, I hated school. Like, I struggled through school. Like, I never had a thing that, like, grabbed me outside of wrestling. Wrestling was a thing that, like, pulled me in, right? But in school, that was the only thing that really, like, stuck with me. But, like, all this is wrestling as an example. So, like, I started loving it. I started geeking out. I started going through the process. I started learning. But eventually, there comes a point in everyone's life where it doesn't matter how much you learn, there comes a spot where, like, you can't continue to progress. It's impossible to continue to progress through learning alone. Eventually, you fill up. Okay? But there's a season of your life where you have to go through this process, right? But after you've filled up and you've gotten everything in there, then the only way for you to continue to progress is to shift from growing yourself to contribution. And then when you shift to contribution, that's where you start growing again. Okay? So, the example from wrestling. I was wrestling in high school. I became good. I was a state champion. I took second place in the country. And I was going to college. I was growing. I was a good wrestler, right? And between my senior year and my first year of college, one of my wrestling coaches, he asked me, he said, hey, do you want to come coach at wrestling camp? I was like, sure. He's like, I'll pay you, I think it was like $150 for the week. I was like, dude, I'll be rich. I'll be awesome. So drove to St. George, Utah, this wrestling camp. I'm there all week. And he asked me to teach these kids these moves. So I'm teaching these kids these moves. I'm like, hey, this is how you do it. Go do it. And they go into, they try to do it. And none of them did it right. I'm like, you morons? Like, you just come back in and show me this is how you do the move. Go and do it. And I'm like, none of them could do it. I'm like, how is this so hard? Like, this is really, and I'm like, OK, how does this work? So I sit down, I'm trying to do the move, and I'm looking at, like, noticing, like, OK, for me to make this move work. And also, I realized the pattern, right? OK, my knee needed to be here. My hips need to be here. And I was like, oh, that's why this thing works. OK, so I taught everyone, I said, come back. Like, this is where your elbow's got to be. This is where you need to be. And I broke down the pattern. And all of a sudden, they all saw that. And I'm like, oh, cool. And then they were able to start doing it. And I realized, like, that's the pattern. I never knew that that was the reason why this worked. And so that's where the pattern, and I started amplifying it. And also, I got better at it, right? And I started, like, by teaching other people is how I got to the next level in my wrestling career. OK, same thing in business. Like, I had business that I was doing sorts of stuff. I was learning, geeking out. But once I started, like, teaching other people, all of a sudden, like, I started from just growing myself to helping other people. That's when it blew everything, blew up for me. OK? And so for most of you guys, like, that's the phase. Now, what's interesting is that most people don't care about marketing and sales when they're doing this first growth phase, right? Like, I didn't care. I remember I was selling a bunch of stuff online. I was selling softwares and some potato guns and things that I had made. And it was cool. And I remember I'd go into this event, and there was a guy on stage. He was talking about split testing. And he was talking about different headline things. And he was like, I split tested the red headline versus the blue headline. And this one increased conversions. I was like, huh, that's kind of weird. So I'm back in my computer and front page. And I changed the headline from red to blue or blue to whatever it was. And I saw like a 60% increase in sales and making that one little tweak. And I was like, what? That doesn't make any sense at all. But I was like, that is cool. I'm like, what else? Huh. Like, what if any other little tricks I can do that dramatically increase my income? So I started studying other people. And I was like, oh, I did this. I'm like, if I try that over there. And I was like, I just made more. I doubled how much money I made by adding that one little idea from that guy. And I heard someone else. And that's when I got excited about marketing. I was like, I can do something over here. I can place it here. I'll send more money. If it falls out at the bottom without any new things. Like I started geeking out on that. That's when I became super passionate about the business. A lot of people come to my world, they're obsessed about something. Anthony DiClameni, for example, he's obsessed with biohacking. He's doing biohacking, all sorts of stuff. But he didn't care about marketing until he got the point where he biohacked himself like perfection. His body is awesome. His mind is awesome. And it's like, I got to help other people with this. Like this is so valuable. I got to help other people. He started helping other people. And that's when he was awesome. Nobody's coming to him. Nobody's giving him money. Nobody cares. And that's when I started learning the marketing. The marketing becomes exciting. So my guess for you guys, if you're here and you're geeking out on the marketing with me, you're probably past the student phase. You're over here where you're trying to start learning how to get this thing out to people. And that's when this part becomes exciting. But for all of us, we go through the same phase. We start with a student and then eventually you come to an expert. Now, a lot of us, for me especially, I never in a billion years imagined me on stage in front of people talking. I was super shy introverted, nervous, rustle with a shaved head and glasses. You want to stay behind a computer. But the stuff that I had, I was so passionate about, I had to get it out. And I had to learn how to get these things. I had to learn how to become somebody who could share these things with other people. So this is, I just want to spend a little time on this because some people think you're going to wake up one day and also you're going to be amazing. The reality is you're going to wake up and at first you're going to suck at this thing. It's hard. First time you're on stage you start talking, it's not easy. First time you try to convince somebody to buy your thing or you're trying to sell something, it's not easy. And a lot of times we, I had someone a little while ago who we showed him our process, like figure out what you need to do and publish every single day and they started doing it and within a week he messaged me, it's not working. I'm like, it's not working. You spend a week on this. I did a podcast every single day for six years before anybody even subscribed to it. Why? Because I had to learn how to get my voice. I had to figure this stuff out. I had to do it over and over and over and over and over again. You got to put in the time to become who you need to be. This is one of my favorite video clips ever. This is from Garrett White. It's probably the only clip he doesn't curse in, I think. I hope he doesn't curse in it. But this is like what I always understand. Like this is the key. So first off, understand no matter what you try in the beginning it's going to suck because you suck. But you'll get better and you'll suck less as you keep doing this and eventually you'll suck less so little that you'll actually be good. But just surrender to the fact that you're going to suck. Okay. You guys okay with that? You all surrender to the fact that eventually the first you're going to suck. So if you are going to become this leader who's going to cause the movement to get people to be inspired to give you money and actually change their life, a lot of us we're not ready yet.
